filters.term: host=172.31.1.88 and apply_overrides=0 and levels=chmlgf and rows=1000 and min_qod=0 and sort-reverse=severity first=1

result.name: phpMyAdmin End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=The phpMyAdmin version on the remote host has reached the End of Life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.|insight=|affected=|impact=An EOL version of phpMyAdmin is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unfixed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.|solution=Update the phpMyAdmin version on the remote host to a still supported version.|vuldetect=Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update the phpMyAdmin version on the remote host to a still supported version.

result.description:
The "phpMyAdmin" version on the remote host has reached the end of life.
CPE: cpe:/a:phpmyadmin:phpmyadmin:4.6.6 Installed version: 4.6.6 Location/URL: / EOL version: 4.6 EOL date: 2017-04-01

result.name: jQuery End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux

nvt.name: jQuery End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=The jQuery version on the remote host has reached the end of life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.|insight=|affected=|impact=An EOL version of jQuery is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unfixed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.|solution=Update jQuery on the remote host to a still supported version.|vuldetect=Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update jQuery on the remote host to a still supported version.

result.description:
The "jQuery" version on the remote host has reached the end of life.
CPE: cpe:/a:jquery:jquery:2.1.4 Installed version: 2.1.4 Location/URL: /js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js EOL version: 2 EOL date: unknown Detection info (see OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.150658 for more info): - Identified file: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js - Referenced at: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/

Unfixed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.|solution=Update jQuery on the remote host to a still supported version.|vuldetect=Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: phpMyAdmin 4.5.0 <= 4.8.4 SQL Injection Vulnerability (PMASA-2019-2) - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin 4.5.0 <= 4.8.4 SQL Injection Vulnerability (PMASA-2019-2) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to an SQL injection vulnerability.|insight=A vulnerability was reported where a specially crafted username can be used to trigger an SQL injection attack through the designer feature.|affected=phpMyAdmin versions 4.5.0 through 4.8.4.|impact=|solution=Update to version 4.8.5.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.8.5 Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.8.6 SQL Injection Vulnerability (PMASA-2019-3) - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.8.6 SQL Injection Vulnerability (PMASA-2019-3) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to an SQL injection vulnerability.|insight=A vulnerability was reported where a specially crafted database name can be used to trigger an SQL injection attack through the designer feature.|affected=phpMyAdmin prior to version 4.8.6.|impact=|solution=Update to version 4.8.6 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.8.6 Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.6, 5.x < 5.0.3 Multiple Vulnerabilities (PMASA-2020-5, PMASA-2020-6) - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.6, 5.x < 5.0.3 Multiple Vulnerabilities (PMASA-2020-5, PMASA-2020-6) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- XSS relating to the transformation feature (CVE-2020-26934)
- SQL injection vulnerability in SearchController (CVE-2020-26935)|affected=phpMyAdmin prior to version 4.9.6 and 5.x prior to 5.0.3.|impact=Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to:
- If an attacker sends a crafted link to the victim with the malicious JavaScript, when the victim clicks on the link, the JavaScript will run and complete the instructions made by the attacker (CVE-2020-26934)
- An attacker could use this flaw to inject malicious SQL in to a query (CVE-2020-26935)|solution=Update to version 4.9.6, 5.0.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.9.6 Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.2 Multiple Vulnerabilities (PMASA-2019-5) - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.2 Multiple Vulnerabilities (PMASA-2019-5) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=phpMyAdmin is prone to multiple vulnerabilities:
- SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2019-18622)
- Certain Git information is not escaped (CVE-2019-19617)|affected=phpMyAdmin prior to version 4.9.2.|impact=|solution=Update to version 4.9.2 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.9.2 Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin <= 5.1.1 CSV Injection Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin <= 5.1.1 CSV Injection Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to a CSV injection vulnerability via Export Section.|insight=NOTE: the vendor disputes this because 'the CSV file is accurately generated based on the database contents.'
Nevertheless the vendor could implement measures while doing the CSV export which could lower or mitigate the risk originating from this flaw.|affected=phpMyAdmin version 5.1.1 and prior.|impact=|solution=No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=WillNotFix
nvt.solution: No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: None Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.4, 5.x < 5.0.1 SQL Injection Vulnerability (PMASA-2020-1) - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.4, 5.x < 5.0.1 SQL Injection Vulnerability (PMASA-2020-1) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to an SQL injection vulnerability.|insight=An SQL injection flaw has been discovered in the user accounts page.|affected=phpMyAdmin prior to version 4.9.4 and 5.x prior to 5.0.1.|impact=A malicious user could inject custom SQL in place of their own username when creating queries to this page. An attacker must have a valid MySQL account to access the server.|solution=Update to version 4.9.4, 5.0.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.9.4 Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.5, 5.x < 5.0.2 Multiple SQL Injection Vulnerabilities (PMASA-2020-2, PMASA-2020-3, PMASA-2020-4) - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.5, 5.x < 5.0.2 Multiple SQL Injection Vulnerabilities (PMASA-2020-2, PMASA-2020-3, PMASA-2020-4) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to multiple SQL injection vulnerabilities.|insight=The following SQL injection vulnerabilities exist:
- An SQL injection vulnerability was found in how phpMyAdmin retrieves the current username
- An SQL injection vulnerability has been discovered where certain parameters are not properly escaped when generating certain queries for search actions within phpMyAdmin
- An SQL injection vulnerability was discovered where malicious code could be used to trigger an XSS attack through retrieving and displaying results.|affected=phpMyAdmin prior to version 4.9.5 and 5.x prior to 5.0.2.|impact=Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to:
- create a specially-crafted username and then trick the victim in to performing specific actions with that user account (such as editing its privileges)
- generate specially-crafted database or table names
- insert specially-crafted data in to certain database tables, which when retrieved (for instance, through the Browse tab) can trigger an XSS attack|solution=Update to version 4.9.5, 5.0.2 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.9.5 Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.10, 5.x < 5.1.3 Information Disclosure Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.10, 5.x < 5.1.3 Information Disclosure Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.|insight=The issue could allow users to cause an error that would reveal the path on disk where phpMyAdmin is running from. This requires the server to be running with display_errors on, which is not the recommended setting for a production environment.|affected=phpMyAdmin prior to version 4.9.10 and version 5.x through 5.1.2.|impact=|solution=Update to version 4.9.10, 5.1.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.9.10 Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.0 CSRF Vulnerability (PMASA-2019-4) - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.0 CSRF Vulnerability (PMASA-2019-4) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to a CSRF vulnerability.|insight=A vulnerability was found that allows an attacker to trigger a CSRF attack against a phpMyAdmin user. The attacker can trick the user, for instance through a broken tag pointing at the victim's phpMyAdmin database, and the attacker can potentially deliver a payload (such as a specific INSERT or DELETE statement) through the victim.|affected=phpMyAdmin prior to version 4.9.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 4.9.0 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.9.0 Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.1 CSRF Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin < 4.9.1 CSRF Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to a CSRF vulnerability.|insight=A CSRF issue in phpMyAdmin allows deletion of any server in the Setup page.|affected=phpMyAdmin version 4.9.0.1 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to phpMyAdmin version 4.9.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to phpMyAdmin version 4.9.1 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.9.1 Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin 4.x < 4.8.4 Multiple Vulnerabilities (PMASA-2018-6, PMASA-2018-8) - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin 4.x < 4.8.4 Multiple Vulnerabilities (PMASA-2018-6, PMASA-2018-8) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to multiple security vulnerabilities.|insight=- A flaw has been found where an attacker can exploit phpMyAdmin to leak the contents of a local file. The attacker must have access to the phpMyAdmin Configuration Storage tables, although these can easily be created in any database to which the attacker has access. An attacker must have valid credentials to log in to phpMyAdmin. This vulnerability does not allow an attacker to circumvent the login system (CVE-2018-19968).
- A Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability was found in the navigation tree, where an attacker can deliver a payload to a user through a specially-crafted database/table name (CVE-2018-19970).|affected=phpMyAdmin versions from at least 4.0 through 4.8.3.|impact=|solution=Update to version 4.8.4 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.8.4 Installation path / port: /

result.name: jQuery 1.0.3 < 3.5.0 XSS Vulnerability

nvt.name: jQuery 1.0.3 < 3.5.0 XSS Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=jQuery is prone to a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability when appending HTML containing option elements.|insight=Passing HTML containing <option> elements from untrusted sources - even after sanitizing them - to one of jQuery's DOM manipulation methods (i.e. .html(), .append(), and others) may execute untrusted code.|affected=jQuery versions starting from 1.0.3 and prior to version 3.5.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.5.0 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description:
Installed version: 2.1.4 Fixed version: 3.5.0 Installation path / port: /js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js
Detection info (see OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.150658 for more info): - Identified file: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js - Referenced at: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/

result.name: jQuery 1.2 < 3.5.0 XSS Vulnerability

nvt.name: jQuery 1.2 < 3.5.0 XSS Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=jQuery is prone to a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in jQuery.htmlPrefilter and related methods.|insight=Passing HTML from untrusted sources - even after sanitizing it - to one of jQuery's DOM manipulation methods (i.e. .html(), .append(), and others) may execute untrusted code.|affected=jQuery versions starting from 1.2 and prior to version 3.5.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.5.0 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description:
Installed version: 2.1.4 Fixed version: 3.5.0 Installation path / port: /js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js
Detection info (see OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.150658 for more info): - Identified file: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js - Referenced at: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/

result.name: jQuery 1.0.3 < 3.5.0 XSS Vulnerability

nvt.name: jQuery 1.0.3 < 3.5.0 XSS Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=jQuery is prone to a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability when appending HTML containing option elements.|insight=Passing HTML containing <option> elements from untrusted sources - even after sanitizing them - to one of jQuery's DOM manipulation methods (i.e. .html(), .append(), and others) may execute untrusted code.|affected=jQuery versions starting from 1.0.3 and prior to version 3.5.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.5.0 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description:
Installed version: 1.11.3 Fixed version: 3.5.0 Installation path / port: /doc/html/_static/jquery.js
Detection info (see OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.150658 for more info): - Identified file: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/_static/jquery.js - Referenced at: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/

nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=jQuery is prone to a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in jQuery.htmlPrefilter and related methods.|insight=Passing HTML from untrusted sources - even after sanitizing it - to one of jQuery's DOM manipulation methods (i.e. .html(), .append(), and others) may execute untrusted code.|affected=jQuery versions starting from 1.2 and prior to version 3.5.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.5.0 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description:
Installed version: 1.11.3 Fixed version: 3.5.0 Installation path / port: /doc/html/_static/jquery.js
Detection info (see OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.150658 for more info): - Identified file: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/_static/jquery.js - Referenced at: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/

result.name: jQuery < 3.0.0 XSS Vulnerability

nvt.name: jQuery < 3.0.0 XSS Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=jQuery is prone to a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability.|insight=When a cross-domain Ajax request is performed without the dataType option, causing text/javascript responses to be executed.|affected=jQuery prior to version 3.0.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.0.0 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description:
Installed version: 2.1.4 Fixed version: 3.0.0 Installation path / port: /js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js
Detection info (see OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.150658 for more info): - Identified file: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js - Referenced at: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/

result.name: jQuery < 3.4.0 Object Extensions Vulnerability

nvt.name: jQuery < 3.4.0 Object Extensions Vulnerability
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=jQuery is prone to multiple vulnerabilities regarding property injection in Object.prototype.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2019-5428: A prototype pollution vulnerability exists that allows an attacker to inject properties on Object.prototype.
- CVE-2019-11358: jQuery mishandles jQuery.extend(true, {}, ...) because of Object.prototype pollution. If an unsanitized source object contained an enumerable __proto__ property, it could extend the native Object.prototype.|affected=jQuery prior to version 3.4.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.4.0 or later. Patch diffs are available for older versions.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 3.4.0 or later. Patch diffs are available for older versions.

result.description:
Installed version: 2.1.4 Fixed version: 3.4.0 Installation path / port: /js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js
Detection info (see OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.150658 for more info): - Identified file: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js - Referenced at: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/

result.name: jQuery < 3.4.0 Object Extensions Vulnerability

nvt.name: jQuery < 3.4.0 Object Extensions Vulnerability
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=jQuery is prone to multiple vulnerabilities regarding property injection in Object.prototype.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2019-5428: A prototype pollution vulnerability exists that allows an attacker to inject properties on Object.prototype.
- CVE-2019-11358: jQuery mishandles jQuery.extend(true, {}, ...) because of Object.prototype pollution. If an unsanitized source object contained an enumerable __proto__ property, it could extend the native Object.prototype.|affected=jQuery prior to version 3.4.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.4.0 or later. Patch diffs are available for older versions.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 3.4.0 or later. Patch diffs are available for older versions.

result.name: phpMyAdmin <= 4.8.2 XSS Vulnerability (PMASA-2018-5) - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin <= 4.8.2 XSS Vulnerability (PMASA-2018-5) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to an authenticated cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability.|insight=An authenticated attacker could trick a user into importing a specially crafted file, resulting in the attacker gaining control over the user's account.|affected=phpMyAdmin through version 4.8.2.|impact=|solution=Update to version 4.8.3.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.8.3

result.name: phpMyAdmin Cross-Site Scripting Vulnerability (PMASA-2018-3) - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin Cross-Site Scripting Vulnerability (PMASA-2018-3) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability.|insight=The flaw exists due to insufficient validation of input passed to 'js/designer/move.js' script in phpMyAdmin.|affected=phpMyAdmin versions prior to 4.8.2 on Linux|impact=Successful exploitation will allow an attacker to inject arbitrary web script or HTML via crafted database name.|solution=Upgrade to version 4.8.2 or newer. Please see the references for more information.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to version 4.8.2 or newer. Please see the references for more information.

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.8.2 Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin 4.0 <= 4.8.4 Arbitrary File Read Vulnerability (PMASA-2019-1) - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin 4.0 <= 4.8.4 Arbitrary File Read Vulnerability (PMASA-2019-1) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to an arbitrary file read vulnerability.|insight=When AllowArbitraryServer configuration set to true, with the use of a rogue MySQL server, an attacker can read any file on the server that the web server's user can access.
phpMyadmin attempts to block the use of LOAD DATA INFILE, but due to a bug in PHP, this check is not honored. Additionally, when using the 'mysql' extension, mysql.allow_local_infile is enabled by default. Both of these conditions allow the attack to occur.|affected=phpMyAdmin versions 4.0 through 4.8.4.|impact=|solution=Update to version 4.8.5.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.8.5 Installation path / port: /

result.name: phpMyAdmin 4.3.x < 4.9.11, 5.2.x < 5.2.1 XSS Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: phpMyAdmin 4.3.x < 4.9.11, 5.2.x < 5.2.1 XSS Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=phpMyAdmin is prone to a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability.|insight=An authenticated user can trigger a cross-site scripting (XSS) attack by uploading a specially-crafted .sql file through the drag-and-drop interface.|affected=phpMyAdmin version 4.3.x through 4.9.10 and 5.2.x prior to 5.2.1.|impact=|solution=Update to version 4.9.11, 5.2.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.6.6 Fixed version: 4.9.11 Installation path / port: /

result.name: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information via HTTP

nvt.name: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information via HTTP
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:A/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:N|summary=The host / application transmits sensitive information (username, passwords) in cleartext via HTTP.|insight=|affected=Hosts / applications which doesn't enforce the transmission of sensitive data via an encrypted SSL/TLS connection.|impact=An attacker could use this situation to compromise or eavesdrop on the HTTP communication between the client and the server using a man-in-the-middle attack to get access to sensitive data like usernames or passwords.|solution=Enforce the transmission of sensitive data via an encrypted SSL/TLS connection. Additionally make sure the host / application is redirecting all users to the secured SSL/TLS connection before allowing to input sensitive data into the mentioned functions.|vuldetect=Evaluate previous collected information and check if the host / application is not enforcing the transmission of sensitive data via an encrypted SSL/TLS connection.
The script is currently checking the following:
- HTTP Basic Authentication (Basic Auth)
- HTTP Forms (e.g. Login) with input field of type 'password'|solution_type=Workaround
nvt.solution: Enforce the transmission of sensitive data via an encrypted SSL/TLS connection. Additionally make sure the host / application is redirecting all users to the secured SSL/TLS connection before allowing to input sensitive data into the mentioned functions.

result.description:
The following input fields were identified (URL:input name):
http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/:pma_password

result.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure

nvt.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.|insight=The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as defined by RFC1323/RFC7323.|affected=TCP implementations that implement RFC1323/RFC7323.|impact=A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.|solution=To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.|vuldetect=Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.

result.description:
It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 2842405620 Packet 2: 2842406743

result.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.|insight=The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.|affected=|impact=This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.|solution=Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)|vuldetect=Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)

result.description: The following response / ICMP packet has been received: - ICMP Type: 14 - ICMP Code: 0

result.name: jQuery Detection Consolidation

nvt.name: jQuery Detection Consolidation
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Consolidation of jQuery detections.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Detected jQuery
Version: 1.11.3 Location: /doc/html/_static/jquery.js CPE: cpe:/a:jquery:jquery:1.11.3
Concluded from version/product identification result: src="_static/jquery.js jQuery JavaScript Library v1.11.3
Concluded from version/product identification location: - Identified file: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/_static/jquery.js - Referenced at: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/
Detected jQuery
Version: 2.1.4 Location: /js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js CPE: cpe:/a:jquery:jquery:2.1.4
Concluded from version/product identification result: src="js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js
Concluded from version/product identification location: - Identified file: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/get_scripts.js.php?scripts%5B%5D=jquery/jquery-2.1.4.min.js - Referenced at: http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/

result.name: Allowed HTTP Methods Enumeration

nvt.name: Allowed HTTP Methods Enumeration
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Enumerates which HTTP methods are allowed.|insight=- Basic HTTP methods: GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, DELETE, CONNECT, OPTIONS, TRACE
- Extended HTTP methods: ACL, BASELINE-CONTROL, BIND, CHECKIN, CHECKOUT, COPY, LABEL, LINK, LOCK, MERGE, MKACTIVITY, MKCALENDAR, MKCOL, MKREDIRECTREF, MKWORKSPACE, MOVE, ORDERPATCH, PATCH, PRI, PROPFIND, PROPPATCH, REBIND, REPORT, SEARCH, UNBIND, UNCHECKOUT, UNLINK, UNLOCK, UPDATE, UPDATEREDIRECTREF, VERSION-CONTROL|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=Sends multiple HTTP requests and checks the responses.
Disclaimer:
- This enumeration script is provided 'as is' (means no support is given) and only providing rudimentary information about the possible enabled methods
- This script doesn't guarantee completeness or full reliability
For more reliable determination of the enabled HTTP methods please inspect the configuration of the web server manually and directly on the target host.|solution_type=

result.description:
The following list contains the URLs and corresponding supported HTTP methods.
URL | HTTP Methods ---------------------------------------------- http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/ | GET,HEAD,POST

result.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting

nvt.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Best matching OS:
OS: Linux Kernel CPE: cpe:/o:linux:kernel Found by VT: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.102002 (Operating System (OS) Detection (ICMP)) Concluded from ICMP based OS fingerprint Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information

result.name: Services

nvt.name: Services
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This plugin performs service detection.|insight=This plugin attempts to guess which service is running on the remote port(s). For instance, it searches for a web server which could listen on another port than 80 or 443 and makes this information available for other check routines.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: A web server is running on this port

result.name: CPE Inventory

nvt.name: CPE Inventory
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.
Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:
After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: 172.31.1.88|cpe:/a:f5:nginx 172.31.1.88|cpe:/a:jquery:jquery:1.11.3 172.31.1.88|cpe:/a:jquery:jquery:2.1.4 172.31.1.88|cpe:/a:nginx:nginx 172.31.1.88|cpe:/a:phpmyadmin:phpmyadmin:4.6.6 172.31.1.88|cpe:/o:linux:kernel

result.name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting

nvt.name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=The script consolidates and reports various information for web application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.
This information is based on the following scripts / settings:
- HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)
- No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)
- Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)
- Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)
- The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use
- The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and 'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the 'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use
If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community forum.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
The Hostname/IP "pma46.trabalho_vulnnet" was used to access the remote host.
Generic web application scanning is disabled for this host via the "Enable generic web application scanning" option within the "Global variable settings" of the scan config in use.
Requests to this service are done via HTTP/1.1.
This service seems to be able to host PHP scripts.
This service seems to be able to host ASP scripts.
The User-Agent "Mozilla/5.0 [en] (X11, U; OpenVAS-VT <version replaced>)" was used to access the remote host.
Historic /scripts and /cgi-bin are not added to the directories used for web application scanning. You can enable this again with the "Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning" option within the "Global variable settings" of the scan config in use.
A possible recursion was detected during web application scanning:
The service is using a relative URL in one or more HTML references where e.g. /file1.html contains <a href="subdir/file2.html"> and a subsequent request for subdir/file2.html is linking to subdir/file2.html. This would resolves to subdir/subdir/file2.html causing a recursion. To work around this counter-measures have been enabled but the service should be fixed as well to not use such problematic links. Below an excerpt of URLs is shown to help identify those issues.
Syntax : URL (HTML link)
http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/ (_static/classic.css) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/ (_static/doctools.js) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/ (_static/jquery.js) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/ (_static/pygments.css) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/ (_static/underscore.js)
The following directories were used for web application scanning:
http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/ http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/_images http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/_sources http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/_static http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/sql
While this is not, in and of itself, a bug, you should manually inspect these directories to ensure that they are in compliance with company security standards
The following directories were excluded from web application scanning because the "Regex pattern to exclude directories from CGI scanning" setting of the VT "Global variable settings" (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.12288) for this scan was: "/(index\.php|image|img|css|js$|js/|javascript|style|theme|icon|jquery|graphic|grafik|picture|bilder|thumbnail|media/|skins?/)"
http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/codemirror/addon/hint http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/codemirror/addon/lint http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/codemirror/lib http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/themes http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/themes/pmahomme/css http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/themes/pmahomme/img http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/themes/pmahomme/jquery
The following CGIs were discovered:
Syntax : cginame (arguments [default value])
http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/doc/html/search.html (check_keywords [yes] q [] area [default] ) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/index.php (token [***replaced***] pma_username [] target [index.php] table [] collation_connection [utf8mb4_unicode_ci] lang [] server [1] db [] pma_password [] ) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/get_image.js.php (theme [pmahomme] v [4.6.6] ) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/get_scripts.js.php (v [4.6.6] scripts%5B%5D [jquery/jquery.ba-hashchange-1.3.js] ) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/messages.php (lang [en] db [] collation_connection [utf8mb4_unicode_ci] token [***replaced***] v [4.6.6] ) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/whitelist.php (lang [en] db [] collation_connection [utf8mb4_unicode_ci] token [***replaced***] v [4.6.6] ) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/phpmyadmin.css.php (nocache [4459207702ltr] ) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/url.php (url [https%3A%2F%2Fwww.phpmyadmin.net%2F] )
The following cgi scripts were excluded from web application scanning because of the "Regex pattern to exclude cgi scripts" setting of the VT "Web mirroring" (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662) for this scan was: "\.(js|css)$"
Syntax : cginame (arguments [default value])
http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/codemirror/addon/hint/show-hint.css (v [4.6.6] ) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/codemirror/addon/lint/lint.css (v [4.6.6] ) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/js/codemirror/lib/codemirror.css (v [4.6.6] ) http://pma46.trabalho_vulnnet/themes/pmahomme/css/printview.css (v [4.6.6] )

result.name: HTTP Server Banner Enumeration

nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This script tries to detect / enumerate different HTTP server banner (e.g. from a frontend, backend or proxy server) by sending various different HTTP requests (valid and invalid ones).|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:

result.name: Traceroute

nvt.name: Traceroute
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.|insight=For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.|solution_type=

result.description:
Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.31.1.88):
172.30.0.2 172.31.1.88
Network distance between scanner and target: 2

result.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check

nvt.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:A/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.|solution_type=

result.description: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an answer which means IP forwarding is enabled.

nvt.name: Generic HTTP Directory Traversal / File Inclusion (Web Root) - Active Check
